free_text: Identification of fission-like events in the 16O+181Ta
system: Mass and isotopic yield distribution
S.V.College, Aligarh; UGC-DAE-CSR, Bidhan
Nagar, Kolkata
15-UD Pelletron accelerator
Results of Tl*, Hg* and Au* isotops given
181Ta and Al (~2.0 mg/cm2) were cut into 1.2 x 1.2 cm2
and were pasted on Al holders having concentric holes
of 1.0 cm diameter.
The thicknesses of the samples were measured at
different locations of the same sample by the alpha-
transmission method.
Beam flux was monitored by an ORTEC current
integrator by taking into account the charge
collected in the Faraday cup
A Precalibrated high-purity germanium gamma-ray
spectrometer of 100 cm3 active volume
To identify reaction residues
The statistical errors in the evaluation of the gamma-
ray intensity and the background subtraction were
different for different observed residues and were
separately evaluated, by employing the peak fitting
software.
The uncertainties of the nuclear data like branching
ratio, decay constant etc., which have been taken
from the Table of Isotopes have not been taken into
consideration.
Considering statistical errors and all the
possible sources of errors as described below, the
uncertainty is estimated to be less than 15%.
the error in the target thickness     (0.1%)
error due to beam fluctuations     (<5%)
uncertainty due to the fitting
of the efficiency curve                       (<3%)
error in the efficiency due to the
solid-angle effect is less than 5%.
error due to the decrease in beam
intensity                                     (<2%)
uncertainty in the dead time of the
spectrometer was kept <10% by suitably adjusting the
sample-detector distance and the corrections for it
were applied to the counting rate.
Tbl. II, III from PR/C,84,014612,2011
Entry approved by Vijay R.Sharma
BT
On. Finalized
The parameters for the isotopic yield distributions
were obtained by fitting the respective production
cross sections to the Gaussian distribution.
Width of isotopic yield distributions for
different observed fission residues
